qas.q: What Regulation does the National Guard use for Officer Promotions and what is the official title?
qas.a: AR 135-155 Promotion of Commissioned Officers and Warrant Officers Other Than General Officers.

qas.q: What is the process of transferring a soldier from one unit to another within the state?
qas.a: First the Soldier gets a letter of acceptance from the gaining unit, then they request from their losing commander to be released for transfer. Once the losing unit has the acceptance letter and the commander's release they complete a DA 4187 requesting transfer through their battalion S1 section.

qas.q: How are documents and information updated in SIDPERS and individual records?
What is a PULHES and what does it stand for?
qas.a: Physical Profile given as the result of a PHA. Pulmonary, Upper, Lower, Hearing, Eyes, pSychiatric.

qas.q: What does IPERMS stand for and what does it do?
qas.a: Interactive Personnel Electronic Records Management System. Allows digital storage of soldier records.

qas.q: What filing system is used within the ARNG?
qas.a: Army Records Information Management System (ARIMS). AR 25-400-2

qas.q: What form has your ASVAB scores?
qas.a: DA 1966. They are also found on ERB/ORB.

qas.q: What is MILPO and what Regulation is it covered in?
qas.a: Military Processing Operations. Used to process military orders (promotions, reductions, transfers, and discharges). The primary regulation related to MILPO is AR 600-8-105 Military Orders.

Enlisted Promotion System. It is used in accordance with the STEP (Select, Train, Educate, Promote) career management model. This model incorporates PME (Professional Military Education) requirements in order to be promoted. (BLC, ALC, etc.)

Retention Management System. (Also see RCMS-G Reserve Component Manpower System-Guard). It allows tracking for unit sponsorship, IST, AWOL recovery, and career development counseling and retention. RMS coincides with GIMS (Guard Incentive Management System) which allows management for incentives and education benefits for Soldiers.

qas.q: What form is used for reenlistment?

qas.a: Reserve Component Automation System. It helps maintain mobilization, safety, personnel and force authorization requirements. It is infrastructure. Used in conjunction with multiple systems including SIDPERS (Standard Installation/Division Personnel System), MEDPROS (Medical Protection System), and MILPO (Military Processing Operations). Essentially RCAS is a readiness tracking system.

qas.q: What form is used for Award recommendations?

qas.a: Report to Suspend Favorable Personnel Actions (Flag). Units reuest flagging action through PAT (Personnel Action Tracker).

qas.q: What form is the Army Physical Fitness Scorecard?

qas.a: Election of Service Group Life Insurance policy.
Force Management System. It basically is prescribed allowances for unit force (personnel) utilizing TDAs, TOEs, MTOEs, CTAs and JTAs.

qas.q: What field manual is used for Army Human Resources Support?
What are the key functions of a 42A?
qas.a: Personnel accountability, strength reporting, retention operations, personnel information management, casualty operations.

qas.q: What is the UMR and what readiness functions pertain to it?
qas.a: Unit Manning Report. Add / Remove Flags, Identify Vacancies, and Move Excess to valid Paragraph and Line number in MTOE (Modified Table of Organizational Equipment)..

qas.q: What Medical operations must a readiness related NCO be responsible for?
qas.a: Review MEDPROS, Prepare MND (Medically Non-Deployable) / Dental Counselings, Review LOD (Line Of Duty injury) Module, Submit Medical Documents, Coordinate Make-up PHA, Contact MND Soldiers.

qas.q: Readiness NCOER actions done in EES (Evaluation Entry System)
qas.a: Submit Completed NCOERs, Review Late NCOER, Contact Raters, Review NCOERs coming Due, Prepare new NCOER shells, Update NCOER Rating Chain.
